steps.title: Know what infinitely many solutions means
steps.content: A system has infinitely many solutions only when both equations are the same line. The second equation must produce exactly the same $y$-values as $y=6x+3$ for every $x$.

steps.title: Use a two-value Desmos table
steps.content: Use two quick $x$-values, $0$ and $1$. For the original equation, the outputs are $3$ and $9$. In Desmos, compare the outputs of the choices after solving each for $y$.

steps.title: Find the matching output list
steps.content: The original list is $y_1=[3,9]$. Choice D gives $d_1=[3,9]$, the exact same output list. The other choices give different outputs, so they represent different lines.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Choice D, $2y=2(6x+3)$, is just the original equation multiplied by $2$, so it describes the same line. The system therefore has infinitely many solutions. Choice D is correct.
